message.content:
КОНТЕКСТ. Монорепо /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite). Также есть ОТДЕЛЬНЫЙ каталог /Users/madiever/edu-pwa (вне репо) — вероятно student-PWA.
На ветке feat/knowledge-bank только что реализованы и запушены изменения (3 коммита поверх 6431bf3):
 Part A (админ-UI банка, аддитивно): новый бэкенд GET /api/v1/ktp/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/items (admin); клиентские ktpApi методы KC/банка + типы; новый компонент client/src/components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx врезан per-topic в client/src/pages/admin/KtpCatalogPage.tsx; мутации гейтятся role==='admin'.
 Part B (размер теста + качество): Test.model валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120 (это ЕДИНАЯ точка валидации сохранения ВСЕХ тестов: regular/ent/bank/trial/solo); assembleNodeTest принимает size (5/10/15/20); /tests/node-bank принимает size; /tests/generate(+guest) получили профиле-зависимую валидацию questionCount (ent: 10..120 кратно10; regular: 1..50); ai.service qc-ошибки переведены на AppError.badRequest (400); генерация банка переписана (раунды+разброс difficulty); дедуп contentHash теперь по тексту+вариантам+ответу (+переходный legacy-дедуп).
ВОПРОС ВЛАДЕЛЬЦА: что делать дальше, нужно ли менять фронт, можно ли деплоить фронт и бэк сейчас.
ТЫ — read-only исследователь (Explore). НИЧЕГО не меняй. Используй git/grep/чтение в ОБОИХ путях (/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP и /Users/madiever/edu-pwa). Возвращай факты с путями, оценку влияния на деплой, нужны ли изменения фронта, риски, рекомендацию.

Дименсия: АДВЕРСАРИАЛЬНЫЙ РИСК СМЕНЫ Test.model ВАЛИДАТОРА (===10 → 1..120) В ПРОДЕ.
Пройди ВСЕ пути сохранения Test в работающей системе и оцени, что изменится: regular, ent (1-в-1 и батч 20/40), bank (assembleNodeTest), trial (trial.service блоки 20/10/10/40), solo, kahoot/socket, guest, claim. Что раньше падало на ===10 и теперь сохранится (например батч-ENT/пробник) — это желаемое или скрытый ранее-мёртвый путь, который теперь активируется и может повести себя неожиданно в проде? Полагается ли где-то аналитика/скоринг/предгенерация/кэш sourceContentHash на ровно 10? Дай вердикт: безопасно ли катить эту правку в прод и что проверить до этого.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
commit f07fdbb94aa35a2d369b5d69d24cae19f6322097
Author: madierzh <yerzhan.madi@bcc.kz>
Date:   Tue Jun 23 12:19:34 2026 +0500

    feat(test): configurable test size + bank question quality
    
    Part B. Removes the hardcoded "exactly 10" and improves bank questions.
    
    B1-B3 (variable size):
    - Test.model validator: questions.length !==10 -> range 1..120 (keeps
      per-question validateEntQuestion). Single gate for all flows; also
      un-breaks batched-ENT (20/40) saves used by the trial flow.
    - assembleNodeTest gains size opt (allowed 5/10/15/20), size mixed into
      the bank reuse hash so different sizes don't collide; refill scales
      minPerKc by size/kcCount.
    - /tests/node-bank accepts size; /generate(+guest) validate questionCount 1..120.
    
    B4 (quality):
    - generateForCoverage: retry rounds (verifier rejects some) + difficulty
      spread across center+/-1 so adaptive selection has material.
    - dedup hash now includes options+correctOption (catches same-options
      rephrasings); bank lenient parser drops questions with non-distinct options.
    - distractor requirements added to the KC generation prompt; BANK_PROMPT_VERSION -> v2.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 server/src/utils/entQuestion.util.ts        |   8 +-
 6 files changed, 142 insertions(+), 86 deletions(-)

Author: madierzh <yerzhan.madi@bcc.kz>

    feat(test): configurable test size + bank question quality
    
    Part B. Removes the hardcoded "exactly 10" and improves bank questions.
    
    B1-B3 (variable size):
    - Test.model validator: questions.length !==10 -> range 1..120 (keeps
      per-question validateEntQuestion). Single gate for all flows; also
      un-breaks batched-ENT (20/40) saves used by the trial flow.
    - assembleNodeTest gains size opt (allowed 5/10/15/20), size mixed into
      the bank reuse hash so different sizes don't collide; refill scales
      minPerKc by size/kcCount.
    - /tests/node-bank accepts size; /generate(+guest) validate questionCount 1..120.
    
    B4 (quality):
    - generateForCoverage: retry rounds (verifier rejects some) + difficulty

    - dedup hash now includes options+correctOption (catches same-options

    - distractor requirements added to the KC generation prompt; BANK_PROMPT_VERSION -> v2.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

input.description: Find all Test creation and sourceContentHash usages

content.text: The 3 `Test.create` paths: questionBank.service.ts:349, test.controller.ts:163 and :443. Let me read these and trace the count logic. Let me also check where "10" is hardcoded for counts/scoring/analytics.

Now the critical finding: `entQuestion.util.ts:402` still has `questions.length !== 10`. This was modified in the commit (the diff showed `entQuestion.util.ts | 8 +-`). Let me read it and the ai.service batch logic, plus trial.service.

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text: The git diff for entQuestion.util.ts returned no output via that invocation — let me retry it properly, since the commit stat said it changed by 8 lines. Let me also read the ai.service.generateTest dispatch (how batched vs single is chosen) and the qc validation.

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server && git show f07fdbb -- 'server/src/utils/entQuestion.util.ts' | head -80; echo "=====EXIT $?====="

content.text:
Now I understand the per-call flow: each individual `generateTest` ENT call always produces exactly 10 (validated by `parseAndValidateEntQuestions` requiring exactly 10), and batching multiplies 10×N. So `entQuestion.util.ts:402 !== 10` is a per-batch (10) check, NOT a per-test check — it's fine because each LLM call generates exactly one batch of 10. The model validator is the only per-document gate.

Let me now look at the `computeTestContentHash` fully, the trial.service save path, the questionBank assembleNodeTest, and where trial assembles 80 questions into one Test.

Important: `trial.service.ts` only builds a **plan** (steps with questionCount 20/10/10/40). It does NOT save a Test itself. Each block is a separate test generated via the normal `/tests/generate` flow with its own `questionCount`. So a trial produces 4 separate Test documents (20, 10, 10, 40), NOT one 80-question test. Let me confirm how the trial blocks are submitted — find the trial flow in the client and how questionCount maps. Let me check the routes validation and the questionBank assembleNodeTest, plus whether anything assumes exactly 10 in scoring/analytics/roadmap/trialTopicMastery.

content.content:
1	import { Router } from 'express';
2	import { body, param } from 'express-validator';
3	import { testController } from '../controllers';
4	import { authenticate, asyncHandler, validate } from '../middlewares';
5	
6	/**
7	 * TEST ROUTES
8	 * Маршруты для работы с тестами
9	 */
10	
11	const router = Router();
12	
13	/**
14	 * Гостевые маршруты (без аутентификации)
15	 */
16	router.post(
17	  '/generate-guest',
18	  [
19	    body('subjectId')
20	      .isMongoId()
21	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID'),
22	    body('bookId')
23	      .isMongoId()
24	      .withMessage('Invalid book ID'),
25	    body('chapterId')
26	      .optional()
27	      .isMongoId()
28	      .withMessage('Invalid chapter ID'),
29	    body('fullBook')
30	      .optional()
31	      .isBoolean()
32	      .withMessage('fullBook must be a boolean'),
33	    body('testProfile')
34	      .optional()
35	      .isIn(['regular', 'ent'])
36	      .withMessage('testProfile must be regular or ent'),
37	    body('questionCount')
38	      .optional()
39	      .isInt({ min: 1, max: 120 })
40	      .withMessage('questionCount must be 1..120')
41	      .bail()
42	      .custom((value, { req }) => {
43	        const qc = Number(value);
44	        const ent = req.body?.testProfile !== 'regular'; // дефолтный профиль — ent
45	        if (ent) {
46	          if (qc < 10 || qc > 120 || qc % 10 !== 0) {
47	            throw new Error('Для ЕНТ questionCount должен быть 10..120 и кратен 10');
48	          }
49	        } else if (qc < 1 || qc > 50) {
50	          throw new Error('Для обычного теста questionCount должен быть 1..50');
51	        }
52	        return true;
53	      })
54	  ],
55	  validate,
56	  asyncHandler(testController.generateTestGuest.bind(testController))
57	);
58	
59	router.post(
60	  '/submit-guest',
61	  [
62	    body('testId')
63	      .isMongoId()
64	      .withMessage('Invalid test ID'),
65	    body('answers')
66	      .isArray({ min: 1 })
67	      .withMessage('Answers must be a non-empty array'),
68	    body('answers.*.questionText')
69	      .trim()
70	      .notEmpty()
71	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a question text'),
72	    body('answers.*.selectedOption')
73	      .trim()
74	      .notEmpty()
75	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a selected option')
76	  ],
77	  validate,
78	  asyncHandler(testController.submitTestGuest.bind(testController))
79	);
80	
81	// Защищённые маршруты
82	router.use(authenticate);
83	
84	/**
85	 * @route   POST /tests/claim-guest
86	 * @desc    Привязать гостевой тест к авторизованному пользователю
87	 * @access  Private (authenticated users)
88	 */
89	router.post(
90	  '/claim-guest',
91	  [
92	    body('testId')
93	      .isMongoId()
94	      .withMessage('Invalid test ID'),
95	    body('answers')
96	      .isArray({ min: 1 })
97	      .withMessage('Answers must be a non-empty array'),
98	    body('answers.*.questionText')
99	      .trim()
100	      .notEmpty()
101	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a question text'),
102	    body('answers.*.selectedOption')
103	      .trim()
104	      .notEmpty()
105	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a selected option')
106	  ],
107	  validate,
108	  asyncHandler(testController.claimGuestTest.bind(testController))
109	);
110	
111	/**
112	 * @route   POST /tests/generate
113	 * @desc    Генерация теста на основе контента
114	 * @access  Private (authenticated users)
115	 */
116	router.post(
117	  '/generate',
118	  [
119	    body('subjectId')
120	      .isMongoId()
121	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID'),
122	    body('bookId')
123	      .isMongoId()
124	      .withMessage('Invalid book ID'),
125	    body('chapterId')
126	      .optional()
127	      .isMongoId()
128	      .withMessage('Invalid chapter ID'),
129	    body('fullBook')
130	      .optional()
131	      .isBoolean()
132	      .withMessage('fullBook must be a boolean'),
133	    body('testProfile')
134	      .optional()
135	      .isIn(['regular', 'ent'])
136	      .withMessage('testProfile must be regular or ent'),
137	    body('questionCount')
138	      .optional()
139	      .isInt({ min: 1, max: 120 })
140	      .withMessage('questionCount must be 1..120')
141	      .bail()
142	      .custom((value, { req }) => {
143	        const qc = Number(value);
144	        const ent = req.body?.testProfile !== 'regular'; // дефолтный профиль — ent
145	        if (ent) {
146	          if (qc < 10 || qc > 120 || qc % 10 !== 0) {
147	            throw new Error('Для ЕНТ questionCount должен быть 10..120 и кратен 10');
148	          }
149	        } else if (qc < 1 || qc > 50) {
150	          throw new Error('Для обычного теста questionCount должен быть 1..50');
151	        }
152	        return true;
153	      })
154	  ],
155	  validate,
156	  asyncHandler(testController.generateTest.bind(testController))
157	);
158	
159	/**
160	 * @route   POST /tests/submit
161	 * @desc    Отправка ответов на тест
162	 * @access  Private (authenticated users)
163	 */
164	router.post(
165	  '/submit',
166	  [
167	    body('testId')
168	      .isMongoId()
169	      .withMessage('Invalid test ID'),
170	    body('answers')
171	      .isArray({ min: 1 })
172	      .withMessage('Answers must be a non-empty array'),
173	    body('answers.*.questionText')
174	      .trim()
175	      .notEmpty()
176	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a question text'),
177	    body('answers.*.selectedOption')
178	      .trim()
179	      .notEmpty()
180	      .withMessage('Each answer must have a selected option'),
181	    body('roadmapNodeId').optional().trim(),
182	    body('roadmapSessionId').optional().trim()
183	  ],
184	  validate,
185	  asyncHandler(testController.submitTest.bind(testController))
186	);
187	
188	/**
189	 * @route   POST /tests/node-bank
190	 * @desc    Собрать тест узла КТП из банка вопросов (покрытие KC + переиспользование)
191	 * @access  Private (authenticated users)
192	 */
193	router.post(
194	  '/node-bank',
195	  [
196	    body('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subject ID'),
197	    body('ktpTopicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid ktpTopicId'),
198	    body('size').optional().isIn([5, 10, 15, 20]).withMessage('size must be one of 5,10,15,20')
199	  ],
200	  validate,
201	  asyncHandler(testController.generateNodeTestFromBank.bind(testController))
202	);
203	
204	router.post(
205	  '/solo/start',
206	  [
207	    body('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subject ID'),
208	    body('bookId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid book ID'),
209	    body('chapterId').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid chapter ID'),
210	    body('fullBook').optional().isBoolean().withMessage('fullBook must be a boolean'),
211	    body('testProfile').optional().isIn(['regular', 'ent']).withMessage('testProfile must be regular or ent'),
212	    body('mode').isIn(['daily_pack', 'practice']).withMessage('mode must be daily_pack or practice')
213	  ],
214	  validate,
215	  asyncHandler(testController.startSoloTest.bind(testController))
216	);
217	
218	router.post(
219	  '/solo/answer',
220	  [
221	    body('soloSessionId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid solo session ID'),
222	    body('questionIndex').isInt({ min: 0 }).withMessage('questionIndex must be a non-negative integer'),
223	    body('selectedOption').optional().isString().withMessage('selectedOption must be a string')
224	  ],
225	  validate,
226	  asyncHandler(testController.submitSoloAnswer.bind(testController))
227	);
228	
229	router.post(
230	  '/solo/finish',
231	  [body('soloSessionId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid solo session ID')],
232	  validate,
233	  asyncHandler(testController.finishSoloTest.bind(testController))
234	);
235	
236	router.get(
237	  '/solo/leaderboard',
238	  asyncHandler(testController.getSoloLeaderboard.bind(testController))
239	);
240	
241	/**
242	 * @route   GET /tests/:id
243	 * @desc    Получить тест по ID (без правильных ответов)
244	 * @access  Private (authenticated users)
245	 */
246	router.get(
247	  '/:id',
248	  [
249	    param('id')
250	      .isMongoId()
251	      .withMessage('Invalid test ID')
252	  ],
253	  validate,
254	  asyncHandler(testController.getTestById.bind(testController))
255	);
256	
257	export default router;
258

content.text: Now I have a complete picture of the server side. Key finding: trial blocks are saved as separate Tests with questionCount 20/10/10/40. Under the OLD `===10` validator, the 20 and 40 blocks would FAIL to save. Let me verify this is a real previously-broken path by checking git history — was the trial flow using questionCount before this commit, or is it newly activated? Let me check the previous version of test.routes and whether questionCount was wired before.

content.text:
This is the critical adversarial finding. Before Part B:
- `ai.service` ALREADY had `generateEntBatchedTest` and `questionCount` plumbing (batched 20/40 generation existed).
- `test.controller` ALREADY read `dto.questionCount` and passed it to `generateTest`.
- BUT `test.routes.ts` had NO `questionCount` validation/wiring — so the field was accepted from the body if sent, but there was no documented/validated path.

So the batched-ENT path was reachable before (if a client sent questionCount=20/40) but the resulting Test (20/40 questions) would FAIL the old `===10` validator at `Test.create`. Let me confirm: did the client/edu-pwa actually send questionCount > 10 before this change (making it a previously-broken live path), or was it dead? Let me check both frontends.

content.text: edu-pwa has NO `questionCount`/trial usage at all — it does not send questionCount and does not drive the trial/batched-ENT path. So the batched-ENT save path is driven only by the GenTestMVP client. Let me confirm the trial flow in the GenTestMVP client actually sends questionCount=20/40 (trialQuestionCount), which would create 20/40 question Tests — the previously-broken path. Let me check TestPage.tsx and TrialEntryPage trial wiring and whether trialQuestionCount feeds into the generate payload.

Confirmed. The GenTestMVP client's trial flow (TrialEntryPage → TestStartPage) sends `questionCount: 20/40` (via trialQuestionCount → genQuestionCount → resolvedQuestionCount) with `testProfile: 'ent'`, `forTrial: true`. The server generates 20/40 questions via `generateEntBatchedTest` and tries to `Test.create` them.

This means: BEFORE this commit, on the trial flow, the History block (20) and profile blocks (40) would generate fine but FAIL at `Test.create` due to `questions.length !== 10`. This was a previously-broken (effectively dead/failing) live path, now un-broken. Let me verify the client's trial UI is current (already deployed) by checking git status of client and whether this client code predates Part B. Let me also check kahoot/socket save path and whether anything assumes exactly 10 in scoring/roadmap/trialTopicMastery/analytics.

input.command: echo "=== Was TestStartPage trial questionCount present BEFORE Part B? ===" && git show 6431bf3:client/src/pages/user/TestStartPage.tsx 2>/dev/null | grep -n "trialQuestionCount\|resolvedQuestionCount\|questionCount" | head; echo "=== client diff in 3 Part-A/B commits ==="; git diff --stat 6431bf3 HEAD -- client/ | tail -30
input.description: Check if client trial questionCount predates Part B and client changes in branch

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src && echo "=== hardcoded /10, *10, ===10 in scoring/roadmap/analytics/socket ===" && grep -rn "questions.length\|/ 10\|\* 10\|=== 10\|>= 10\|<= 10\|totalQuestions\|length === " controllers/ services/ socket/ utils/ --include="*.ts" | grep -viE "slice\(0, 10\)|toISOString|\.test\.ts|maxlength|minlength|max: |min: " | head -50

content.text:
Excellent. Two crucial confirmations:

1. The client trial flow (`trialQuestionCount` → questionCount 20/40) **already existed at 6431bf3** (before Part B). So the client has been sending 20/40 to the server, which generated them but FAILED at the old `===10` save validator. This is a confirmed previously-broken live path now un-broken — desired.

2. All scoring/grading is **fully dynamic** (`test.questions.length`, `correctCount / totalQuestions`, `correctCount / totalCount`). No hardcoded "÷10" anywhere in scoring/roadmap/trialTopicMastery. `ai.service:328 batches = total/10` is the batched generation (each batch is exactly 10), not a scoring assumption. `trialTopicMastery.util:20` guards `questions.length !== userAnswers.length` (dynamic, safe).

Let me verify the socket/kahoot save path uses Test (not creating new Tests with fixed size) and check the daily_pack/solo generation count, plus confirm there's no pre-generation cron/script relying on exactly 10.

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src && echo "=== socket/kahoot: any Test.create or fixed count? ===" && grep -n "Test.create\|new Test\|questionCount\|generateTest\|\.length" socket/index.ts services/liveKahoot.service.ts 2>/dev/null | head -30; echo "=== pregeneration / cron / scripts referencing test size 10 ==="; grep -rn "generateTest\|Test.create\|questionCount\|pregenerat\|pre-generat\|cron\|schedule" services/*.ts scripts/*.ts jobs/*.ts 2>/dev/null | grep -viE "computeTestContentHash|\.test\.ts" | head -30

content.text:
Confirmed. Kahoot/socket consume an already-existing Test by `_id` (`room.test`, `testDoc`) and grade purely by `test.questions.length` — they never create a Test with a fixed size. No cron/pregeneration job creates Tests at all. The daily_pack/solo path (test.controller:441) calls `generateTest` with no questionCount → defaults to exactly 10, so solo/daily_pack is unaffected.

Let me do a final verification on two adversarial angles: (1) the cache hash collision — does the new `bank:size:` hash and the `v:3/v:4` ENT hash avoid collisions across sizes; and (2) confirm there are no OTHER callers of `parseAndValidateEntQuestions` that could now receive non-10 arrays. Also confirm the entQuestion.util commit change (the diff returned empty — likely the change was elsewhere in that file).

input.command: cd /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server && echo "=== callers of parseAndValidateEntQuestions ===" && grep -rn "parseAndValidateEntQuestions" server/src --include="*.ts"; echo "=== what changed in entQuestion.util.ts in f07fdbb (full) ==="; git show f07fdbb -- server/src/utils/entQuestion.util.ts | grep -E "^[-+]" | grep -v "^[-+][-+]" | head -40
input.description: Find ENT validator callers and exact util diff

content.content:
=== callers of parseAndValidateEntQuestions ===
ugrep: warning: server/src: No such file or directory
=== what changed in entQuestion.util.ts in f07fdbb (full) ===

content.text: The path was off (cwd is the repo root, server/ prefix needed). Let me redo correctly and check the entQuestion.util change via line-level diff.

input.command: echo "=== callers parseAndValidateEntQuestions ===" && grep -rn "parseAndValidateEntQuestions" server/src --include="*.ts"; echo "=== entQuestion.util diff lines in f07fdbb ==="; git diff 6431bf3 f07fdbb -- server/src/utils/entQuestion.util.ts | grep -E "^[+-]" | grep -vE "^[+-][+-]" | head -40
input.description: Find ENT validator callers and exact util diff

content.text:
Final confirmation. The `entQuestion.util.ts:402 !== 10` is only called from `ai.service.ts:282` — and only within a single per-batch generation where `expectedN` is already gated to 10 for ENT (line 261 enforces `n !== expectedN` rejection first). The util change in Part B was an unrelated bank-quality tweak (distinct-options check in `parseAndValidateRegularQuestions`), NOT a change to the `!== 10` line. So the per-batch ENT generation still correctly requires exactly 10 per LLM call, and batching assembles 20/40/etc. The only per-document size gate is the model validator, which now correctly accepts 1..120.

I have a complete picture. Let me produce the verdict.

input.area: Адверсариальный риск смены валидатора Test.model (questions.length ===10 → 1..120) в проде; все пути сохранения Test
facts: Единственная точка размер-валидации документа Test — server/src/models/Test.model.ts:112-126: было `length !== 10`, стало `length < 1 || length > 120` (+ per-question validateEntQuestion сохранён). Это единственный per-document size-gate для ВСЕХ путей.

Всего 3 пути, реально создающих Test.create: (1) test.controller.ts:163 findOrCreateTest (regular/ent/guest/trial); (2) test.controller.ts:443 startSoloTest (daily_pack/practice); (3) questionBank.service.ts:349 assembleNodeTest (bank). Других `new Test`/insertMany нет.

КРИТИЧНО (ранее-падавший путь, теперь активируется): trial-блоки сохраняются как ОТДЕЛЬНЫЕ Test'ы размером 20/10/10/40, НЕ один тест на 80. trial.service.ts строит только plan (steps c questionCount 20/10/10/40), не сохраняет ничего. Каждый блок идёт через обычный /tests/generate с своим questionCount.

Клиент GenTestMVP уже ДО Part B (на 6431bf3) слал questionCount=20/40 в trial-флоу: client/src/pages/user/TestStartPage.tsx:69-80,157-175 (trialQuestionCount→genQuestionCount→resolvedQuestionCount, testProfile:'ent', forTrial:true). Проверено: эти строки присутствуют в `git show 6431bf3:.../TestStartPage.tsx`.

Серверная обвязка batched-ENT тоже существовала ДО Part B: на 6431bf3 ai.service уже имел generateEntBatchedTest + questionCount (batches=total/10), test.controller уже читал dto.questionCount. НО test.routes.ts на 6431bf3 НЕ имел валидации questionCount. Вывод: блоки 20/40 генерировались, но `Test.create` падал на `=== 10` → trial-блоки >10 вопросов были фактически сломаны в проде. Теперь они сохраняются — это ЖЕЛАЕМОЕ исправление, не новый тёмный путь.

Per-batch ENT-генерация по-прежнему строго=10: ai.service.ts:261 отбрасывает ответ при n!==expectedN (expectedN для ent=10), parseAndValidateEntQuestions (entQuestion.util.ts:402 `!== 10`) вызывается ТОЛЬКО из ai.service.ts:282 на один батч. Изменение util в Part B (distinct-options в parseAndValidateRegularQuestions) к строке `!==10` отношения не имеет.

Скоринг/грейдинг полностью динамический по test.questions.length — нет хардкода ÷10: test.controller.ts:201,217-220 (checkAnswers), 564/595-597 (solo finish); socket/index.ts:314-360 и liveKahoot.service.ts:131,198,276,306 (kahoot грейдит существующий Test по _id, новых Test не создаёт); utils/trialTopicMastery.util.ts:20 guard `questions.length !== userAnswers.length` (динамический).

Кэш sourceContentHash size-aware и без коллизий между размерами: ai.service.ts:608-619 computeTestContentHash включает n=questionCount (v3 для ent>10, v4 иначе); questionBank.service.ts:319-323 префиксует `bank:${size}:` и подмешивает size в hash; solo использует dailyPackId. Аналитики/предгенерации/cron, завязанных на ровно 10, не найдено (нет jobs/, нет Test.create в scripts/).
edu-pwa (/Users/madiever/edu-pwa, ветка feat/edu-app-decomposed-be) НЕ использует questionCount/trial/node-bank вовсе (grep пусто). На batched-ENT/bank-флоу не влияет, регресса от правки не получает.

Серверная валидация теперь согласована с моделью: test.routes.ts:37-53 и 137-153 (ent: 1..120 кратно 10; regular: 1..50), ai.service.ts:75-85 (ent qc%10, regular 1..50). Все они уже отрезают неподходящие размеры ДО Test.create.
frontendChangesNeeded: Для самой правки валидатора — НЕТ обязательных изменений фронта. Клиент GenTestMVP уже слал 20/40 и формировал trial-плrecisely под эту правку; фронт не меняли в этих 3 коммитах кроме админ-банка (client diff: только ktp.api.ts, TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx, KtpCatalogPage.tsx, ktp.types.ts).

Опционально (не блокер): новый POST /tests/node-bank c size и UI выбора размера теста из банка фронтом GenTestMVP пока не вызывается — проверить, нужен ли UI-триггер; без него фича банка-теста просто недоступна из UI, но регресса нет.
deployImpact: Бэк безопасно катить — правка АДДИТИВНА и РАСШИРЯЕТ принимаемый диапазон (10 ⊂ 1..120). Ни один ранее-сохранявшийся 10-вопросный тест не ломается; ломавшиеся 20/40 trial-блоки начинают сохраняться.
Порядок: сначала/одновременно бэк (он ослабляет ограничение и чинит trial). Фронт GenTestMVP можно катить независимо — он уже совместим; админ-банк (Part A) аддитивен и гейтится role==='admin'.
Регресса для regular/solo/daily_pack/kahoot/guest НЕ ожидается: solo/daily_pack по-прежнему дефолтит к 10 (generateTest без questionCount), kahoot грейдит существующий Test динамически.
risks: Качество, а не сохранность: trial-блоки 20/40 теперь РЕАЛЬНО доходят до пользователя (раньше падали на сохранении). Если batched-ENT генерация даёт слабые/дублирующиеся вопросы между сериями — это станет видимым в проде впервые. Проверить фактическое качество батча 40 на проде до широкого включения.

Стоимость/латентность LLM: 40-вопросный профильный блок = 4 последовательных LLM-вызова (batches=total/10) с ретраями; раньше падал быстро на валидаторе. Теперь полный путь активен → выше время ответа и расход токенов на trial. Убедиться в таймаутах/очереди генерации.

Очень большой тест (до 120 = 12 батчей) теперь валиден на уровне модели и маршрутов. Хотя UI ограничивает ввод, прямой API-вызов /tests/generate с questionCount=120 создаст дорогой 12-вызовный прогон. Рейтлимита на размер по стоимости в коде не видно.

Старые «битые» trial-сессии: если в проде есть незавершённые trial-флоу, где блок >10 не создавался, поведение клиента при ретрае теперь иное (тест создастся). Низкий риск, но стоит прогнать сквозной trial e2e после деплоя.

Кэш-ключи новой версии (v3/v4, bank:size:) — старые кэшированные тесты под прежними ключами просто не переиспользуются и догенерируются заново (миграция не нужна, но первый прогон после деплоя дороже).
input.recommendation: Безопасно катить бэк и фронт сейчас. Правка валидатора ===10→1..120 — аддитивное расширение и фактический ФИКС ранее-сломанного prod-пути (trial-блоки 20/40, которые клиент уже слал, но сервер не мог сохранить из-за `=== 10`). Это не активация тёмного мёртвого пути, а починка живого. Все пути сохранения проверены: regular/guest/solo/daily_pack дефолтят к 10 и не меняются; bank (assembleNodeTest) и trial-блоки 20/40 теперь корректно сохраняются; kahoot/socket Test'ы не создают и грейдят динамически; per-batch ENT по-прежнему строго=10. Скоринг/роадмап/trialTopicMastery полностью динамичны — хардкода ÷10 нет. Фронт GenTestMVP менять для этой правки НЕ нужно (он уже совместим); edu-pwa не затронут. ПЕРЕД деплоем проверить: (1) сквозной trial e2e — что блоки История(20) и профиль(40) реально генерируются, сохраняются и грейдятся; (2) качество/латентность/стоимость 40-вопросного батча на проде; (3) при желании — добавить серверный лимит на максимальный размер по стоимости, т.к. прямой API даёт до 120 (12 LLM-вызовов).

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
Я завершил адверсариальное исследование. Краткий вердикт:

**Безопасно катить и бэк, и фронт сейчас.**

Главная находка: правка валидатора `Test.model` (`=== 10` → `1..120`) — это не активация скрытого мёртвого пути, а **починка реально сломанного prod-пути**. Клиент GenTestMVP уже до Part B (на 6431bf3) слал `questionCount=20/40` в trial-флоу (`TestStartPage.tsx`), серверная batched-ENT генерация уже существовала, но `Test.create` падал на `questions.length !== 10` — то есть trial-блоки «История» (20) и профильные (40) генерировались, но не сохранялись. Теперь сохраняются — это желаемое.

Проверены ВСЕ пути сохранения (только 3 реальных `Test.create`):
- regular/guest/solo/daily_pack — дефолтят к 10, поведение не меняется;
- trial-блоки 20/10/10/40 — каждый сохраняется как отдельный Test (не один на 80); trial.service строит только plan, ничего не пишет;
- bank (`assembleNodeTest`) — size-aware, без коллизий в хэше.

Скоринг/грейдинг/роадмап/trialTopicMastery/kahoot полностью динамичны по `test.questions.length` — хардкода ÷10 нет. Kahoot/socket новые Test'ы не создают. Per-batch ENT по-прежнему строго=10. edu-pwa не использует эти пути вообще — регресса не получает.

**Фронт менять не нужно** для этой правки. Перед деплоем стоит прогнать сквозной trial e2e и оценить качество/стоимость/латентность 40-вопросного батча на проде (4 последовательных LLM-вызова), плюс при желании ограничить максимальный размер по стоимости (прямой API допускает до 120 = 12 вызовов).

Детальные факты, риски и порядок деплоя переданы через структурированный вывод.
